1.name: Hearing Aid & Ear Instruments
1.description: Hearing Aid & Ear Instruments. This collection contains a wide range of medical devices used for hearing assistance and ear examination, including hearing aids, ear specula, thermometers and a variety of surgical tools. There are about one thousand one hundred items in this group.

2.name: Dental surgical implant tools
2.description: Dental surgical implant tools are the specialised instruments and supplies that dentists use to place artificial tooth roots into the jawbone. They include small surgical drills, bone graft materials, impression trays and various protective gloves. The collection contains about three thousand nine hundred items, offering a comprehensive range for every implant procedure.

4.description: General Surgical Supplies. This collection contains a wide range of everyday tools and materials that surgeons use to perform operations safely and efficiently, from simple tubes and sutures to specialised implants and diagnostic devices. It comprises about two thousand nine hundred distinct items.

9.name: Implant system accessories
9.description: Implant system accessories are essential tools that complement the main dental implants, helping surgeons to prepare bone, secure the implant, and provide the necessary support during surgery. They include a wide range of items from drilling guides and bone expanders to irrigation sets and special surgical trays. The collection contains about one thousand six hundred unique items, reflecting the breadth of choices available to clinicians.

13.name: Orthopedic Instruments & Implants
13.description: Orthopedic Instruments & Implants is a collection of tools and devices that surgeons use to diagnose, treat and support bone and joint conditions. The items range from specialised plates and drills to ergonomic supports that help doctors perform procedures with precision and care. There are about three thousand two hundred items in this group, offering a comprehensive array of options for a wide variety of medical needs.

14.name: Polyaxial Cannulated Trial Plates
14.description: Polyaxial Cannulated Trial Plates. There are about one thousand one hundred items in this collection.

15.name: Polyaxial Cannulated Pedicle Screws
15.description: Polyaxial Cannulated Pedicle Screws are specialised screws designed to anchor bone grafts and implants into the spine, allowing the screw to be placed at a slight angle to maximise stability while reducing the risk of damage to surrounding tissues. This collection contains roughly six thousand distinct items, each engineered to fit a range of clinical needs across spinal surgery.

16.name: Surgical instruments & consumables
16.description: Surgical instruments & consumables are the tools and disposables that surgeons use during operations. These items range from delicate scissors and forceps to specialised devices and sterile gloves, all designed to support the precise and safe execution of medical procedures. There are about two thousand nine hundred different items in this collection, offering a vast range of options for the operating theatre.

17.name: Orthopedic Surgical Accessories
17.description: Orthopedic Surgical Accessories comprise a vast array of tools and devices that aid surgeons in stabilising bones, joints and soft tissues during operations. These items range from simple braces and plates to custom made compresses and components that provide precise support in the operating theatre. The collection includes about three thousand distinct products, each engineered to meet the exact demands of modern orthopaedic surgery.

18.description: Medline Surgical Supplies is a collection that includes a wide range of items used by medical and surgical teams, from everyday protective equipment to specialised kits designed for particular procedures. 19.name: Ankle‑Foot Orthoses (AFO)
19.description: Ankle‑Foot Orthoses (AFO). These are supportive devices that are fitted to the ankle and foot to help manage pain, improve mobility and provide stability for people with a range of conditions, from arthritis to post‑injury recovery. The collection contains about five hundred different models, each designed to fit a specific need or body type.

20.name: Spinal Interbody Cages
20.description: Spinal Interbody Cages. These are the devices that surgeons use to bridge gaps in the spinal column, providing support and stability during recovery. The collection contains about two thousand nine hundred different variations, each designed to fit a particular patient need.

21.name: Cannulated surgical instruments
21.description: Cannulated surgical instruments are specialised tools that allow surgeons to thread them through body cavities, creating a clear pathway for other devices. They include a range of flexible tubes, stents and catheters used in many areas from urology to orthopaedics. This collection contains roughly about two thousand one hundred distinct items.
